data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Spotify uses a microservices architecture allowing different parts of the application to be independently developed and scaled.
data.technical_spec: Deployed via Docker containers, managed through Kubernetes.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Utilized to efficiently deliver music content across the globe.
data.technical_spec: AWS CloudFront and Akamai are commonly used.

data.title: Event-Driven Applications
data.description: System uses event-driven patterns to manage notifications and real-time updates.
data.technical_spec: Kafka is often used for this purpose.

data.title: NoSQL Database Systems
data.description: Designed to handle vast amounts of music metadata and user information.

data.title: API Gateway
data.description: Central gateway for managing API requests.
data.technical_spec: Based on frameworks like NGINX or Kong.
